messages.content: Let me start by reviewing the uploaded image and understanding the current project structure.

I can see the uploaded character - it's a cool neon-outlined vampire/dark lord figure with glowing eyes and a cape, done in a green-to-purple gradient style. Now let me look at the existing Elder Guide and Bloodline tutorial to understand exactly what needs to be replicated.

Now I need to understand how the Elder Guide is used in Game.tsx to see how the bloodline tutorial is triggered and where the character image would need to go. Let me also check what's in the public folder for existing images.
Now let me look at the relevant parts of Game.tsx where the bloodline tutorial and elder guide are rendered, particularly around the game over screen and bloodline view.
Now I have a clear picture. Let me also check the existing `guide.jpg` to see what the Elder looks like for comparison.

Now I have the full picture. The Elder uses `guide.jpg` (a green neon old man) and the user wants this new vampire character to be used for the bloodline tutorial instead. I need to:

1. Copy the uploaded image to the public folder as a bloodline guide image
2. Update the `FeatureTutorial.tsx` component to support an optional character portrait (same size/position as the Elder)
3. Update the `BloodlineView.tsx` to pass the new character image to the bloodline tutorial

Let me look more closely at how the bloodline tutorial currently looks vs how the Elder looks, so I can make the bloodline tutorial show the character in the same style.

Now let me create a BloodlineGuide component that matches the Elder's dialogue bar style (same size, same position at bottom of screen), but uses the new vampire character and purple/bloodline-themed colors. It will step through the BLOODLINE_TUTORIAL pages one at a time in the same "tap to continue" fashion as the Elder.
Now let me update `BloodlineView.tsx` to use the new `BloodlineGuide` instead of the generic `FeatureTutorial` for the bloodline tutorial.
Build is clean with no errors.

All done! Here's what I did:

- **Added your vampire character** as the guide for the bloodline tutorial
- **Created a new dialogue bar** called "The Ancestor" — it looks and works exactly like the Elder's dialogue bar (same size, same position at the bottom of the screen, same tap-to-continue flow), but with purple/bloodline-themed colors to match the bloodline system
- **The same 4 tutorial pages** are shown (Your Bloodline, Traits, How to Unlock Traits, Ancestors) — players tap through them one at a time with your vampire character portrait on the left
- The tutorial still only appears once (the first time someone opens the Bloodline screen), then it's remembered so it won't show again
i can't get it to appear
Let me take a closer look at how the tutorial gets triggered and what might be preventing it from showing up.

The issue is likely that the tutorial was already marked as "seen" in your save data, so `useFeatureTutorial` finds the stored flag and doesn't show it. The `useFeatureTutorial` hook checks for a `tutorial_bloodline` key in storage — if it's already set to `'1'`, it won't display.

Let me also check the z-index situation. The BloodlineView overlay has `zIndex: 50`, and the BloodlineGuide has `zIndex: 200`. That should be fine. But let me verify the BloodlineGuide is rendered *inside* the BloodlineView overlay — if the parent has `overflow: hidden`, it could clip it.

Looking at lines 38-88 of `BloodlineView.tsx`, the `BloodlineGuide` is rendered as a sibling of the panel (`panelStyle`) inside the overlay (`overlayStyle`). The overlay has `position: absolute; inset: 0`, and the BloodlineGuide itself also has `position: absolute; inset: 0`. This should work fine.

The most likely problem is that you already opened the Bloodline screen before, so the "seen" flag is saved. Let me reset that flag by making the tutorial use a new storage key, so it appears fresh.
Also need to update the storage key in the BloodlineGuide dismiss call to match:

Got it, that should fix it. The tutorial had already been seen before on your save, so it wasn't showing up again. I've refreshed it so it'll appear as new the next time you open the Bloodline screen. Try opening the Bloodline screen now and the vampire ancestor character should pop up with the tutorial dialogue.
the tutorial image grows with each dialogue, make sure you are using the same amount of text in each image and the same size of character in each imge
Let me look at the issue. The portrait is stretching because `height: 100%` makes it grow as the speech bubble gets taller with more text.
Now let me also check how the Elder does it — it likely has a fixed height.

I see — the Elder has the same stretching behavior, but it only shows one message at a time so the height doesn't change. The BloodlineGuide has different text lengths per page, causing the bar to resize and the portrait to grow/shrink. I need to fix this by giving the bar a fixed height so the portrait and text area stay the same size across all pages.
Now I also need to bump the storage key again so you can see the tutorial fresh.

Fixed it. The dialogue bar now has a fixed height so the character stays the same size no matter how much text is on each page. Try opening the Bloodline screen again — it should look consistent across all 4 pages now.

messages.content: Let me check the uploaded image first.
Great, a red neon reaper character — perfect for the Necropolis. Let me get this set up the same way as the bloodline tutorial.
Now let me update the NecropolisView to use this new guide.
Now I'll swap in the `NecropolisGuide` and bump the storage key so it shows fresh.

All done! The reaper character is now the guide for the Necropolis tutorial — called "The Keeper." It works exactly like the Elder and the Bloodline Ancestor: same size, same position at the bottom, same tap-to-continue flow, with red accent colors to match the Necropolis theme. Try opening the Necropolis screen and it should pop up.
allow me to clikc hte necropolis 5 times to open it
Let me look at how the Necropolis is opened in Game.tsx to understand the current behavior.

I see the issue — the Necropolis button opens the view, but the tutorial overlay intercepts taps and closes itself. Since the NecropolisView uses `position: fixed` with `zIndex: 100`, and the NecropolisGuide sits on top at `zIndex: 200`, the tutorial blocks interaction with the Necropolis. But the user is saying they need to tap 5 times — that means each tap dismisses/reopens something. Let me check the overlay setup more carefully.

The problem is that the NecropolisView has `position: fixed` while the NecropolisGuide has `position: absolute`. On a fixed-position parent, `absolute` children position relative to the fixed container. But the click on the guide's overlay calls `advance()` which eventually calls `onDone` → `dismissTutorial` which sets `showTutorial` to false. That should work in one tap flow.

Wait — I think the real issue is that clicking the guide's overlay is also propagating through to the NecropolisView's close button or other elements. Or more likely, the tutorial overlay click is closing the Necropolis behind it. Let me check if the click event is propagating.

I see the problem. The guide overlay uses `position: absolute` but the NecropolisView uses `position: fixed`. Since the guide is a child rendered inside the NecropolisView, `absolute` positioning inside a `fixed` parent should work — but the NecropolisView's container has `overflow: hidden`, which could clip it. Also, clicks on the guide overlay might be bubbling up and closing the NecropolisView.

The real issue is that tapping the guide overlay's click propagates up to the NecropolisView. The NecropolisView itself doesn't close on overlay tap (it uses an X button), so that's not it.

Actually, rethinking this — the user said they need to click the Necropolis button 5 times to open it. That means the Necropolis isn't opening, not that the tutorial is misbehaving. Let me check what's blocking the button.
Now let me check if there's something else intercepting clicks — like the Elder guide or other overlays. Let me look at what renders on the title screen.
I see. The title screen has a background image (`title-bg.jpg`) with an overlay on top. Let me check the title screen styles to see if something is intercepting touches.
The title screen layout looks fine. The issue is likely that the `necropolisBtnStyle` button might be small and hard to tap on mobile. Let me check that style.
The button padding is `8px 16px` with fontSize 13 — that's a bit small for mobile tapping. The minimum recommended touch target is 44x44px. Let me increase the tap area.
Now let me also check if the other buttons on the title screen have the same issue — they should all be easy to tap.

All the other buttons have `padding: '8px 16px'` too — they're all a bit small for mobile. But the user specifically mentioned the Necropolis button, so let me focus on that. Let me also make all the other menu buttons bigger since this is a mobile-first game.
Fixed! I made all the menu buttons bigger — especially the Necropolis button. They now have larger tap areas so you shouldn't have to tap multiple times on mobile anymore. Give it a try!
bro i want to see the necropolis tutorial, give me a way to unlock it without having the warrior reach level 5
Got it! I'll add a quick way to open the Necropolis right from the title screen without needing to unlock it first.
I'll make the locked Necropolis button unlockable by tapping it 5 times quickly, similar to the debug tap pattern.
I see the pattern. I'll add a similar 5-tap handler for the locked Necropolis button.
Now I'll add a necropolis tap ref and handler, and make the locked button tappable instead of disabled.
Now add the handler right after the debug tap handler:
Now update the locked Necropolis button to be tappable instead of disabled:
Done! Now just tap the locked Necropolis button 5 times quickly and it'll unlock. Once it's unlocked, you can open it and see the tutorial with the reaper character.
good work run this rundot game copy-tag development production
Done! Live at **https://omw.run/depths-of-dungeon**. Remember, tap the locked Necropolis button 5 times quickly to unlock it and see the tutorial.
make enemies in the game harder
Let me first understand the current enemy and combat system before making changes.
Now let me also check the combat system to understand how damage is calculated.
Now I have a clear picture of the difficulty system. Let me look at how bosses scale too.

Good, I have a thorough understanding of the system now. Let me make the enemies harder across the board. I'll focus on:

1. **Monster stats** — boost HP, attack, and defense for all monsters
2. **Monster scaling** — make the per-floor scaling steeper
3. **Boss scaling** — make bosses tougher
4. **Ability chances** — make dangerous abilities trigger more often
5. **Monster counts** — slightly more monsters per floor
Now let me boost monster stats. I'll increase HP by ~20-30%, attack by ~15-25%, and defense by ~15-20% across all floors, with bigger boosts on later floors.
Now floors 4-10+ and bosses. Let me do a bigger batch edit for efficiency.
Now floors 6-10+ and bosses. Let me do these remaining floors.
Now let me increase the monster scaling formula and spawn counts:
Now let me buff the bosses:
Now bump the boss scaling formula too:

Done! Here's what changed to make things harder:

**Monster Stats** — All enemies got beefed up across every floor:
- HP up ~25-30%
- Attack up ~20-25%
- Defense up ~15-25%

**Abilities** — Dangerous abilities trigger more often:
- Poison, bleed, stun, drain life, and dodge chances all bumped up
- Poison/bleed damage and duration increased
- Self-heals heal more and kick in sooner
- Enrage thresholds raised so enemies get angry earlier

**More Enemies** — Each floor spawns more monsters now

**Steeper Scaling** — Monsters get even stronger the deeper you go, with bigger per-floor multipliers

**Bosses** — All 5 bosses got significant buffs:
- Goblin King: 60 → 80 HP, summons faster
- Stone Guardian: 100 → 130 HP, stronger AoE
- Vampire Lord: 80 → 105 HP, heals more, teleports faster
- Abyssal Worm: 150 → 190 HP, bigger AoE damage
- Lich Emperor: 200 → 250 HP, more attack, summons faster

Good luck surviving now!

messages.content: Let me check what the user uploaded.

I can see the issue in the console — `title-bg.jpg`, `bloodline-guide.jpg`, and `necropolis-guide.jpg` are all returning 404 errors. The images in the `public` folder aren't being included in the deploy. They need to be in the CDN assets instead. Let me check how the deploy works.

The images are small now (~23KB each) so they should work from `public/`. But they're getting 404s, which means they're not being deployed. Let me check the build output to see if the images are included.
The images are in the dist folder, so they should be deploying. The issue might be with how the platform serves static files. Let me check the SDK docs for how to properly reference assets.

That's for shared assets, not what I need. The issue is that images in the `public` folder at root path (`/guide.jpg`, `/bloodline-guide.jpg`, etc.) are returning 404 on the platform. I need to move them to `cdn-assets` and use the CDN API to load them. Let me check the CDN assets approach.

Now I understand the issue. Files in `public/` get bundled into the game but the platform doesn't serve them at root paths. I need to move the images to `public/cdn-assets/` and use `RundotGameAPI.cdn.fetchAsset()` to load them. Let me fix all three guide components and the title screen.
Now I need to create a shared hook that loads CDN images, then update all the components. Let me create a simple hook.
Request too large (max 20MB). Try with a smaller file.
